section_label: NICU batch 6 — antifibrinolytic, urea cycle, MDR antibiotics, antivirals, vasoactive, anticonvulsant, ROP, RSV prophylaxis, miscellaneous
review_disclaimer: DRAFT — pending clinician review. Verify every dose against your local protocol and current vial concentration before administration.

drugs.monitoring: PT/aPTT, fibrinogen, D-dimer, evidence of thrombus on ECMO circuit, RFT.
drugs.adverse_effects: Thrombosis (especially on stagnant catheters), hypotension, hyperkalaemia.
drugs.contraindications: DIC without concurrent anticoagulation, active intravascular thrombus, severe renal impairment.

review.notes: Tranexamic acid is an alternative antifibrinolytic.

drugs.category: Urea cycle drug — nitrogen pathway substrate

doses.indication: Acute hyperammonaemia (urea cycle defect, pending diagnosis)
doses.dose_per_kg_per_min_starting: 600 mg/kg/day continuous infusion (or 250 mg/kg/day with bolus regimen)
doses.comments: Always co-administer with sodium phenylacetate + sodium benzoate (Ammonul) and dextrose for caloric load.

drugs.monitoring: Plasma ammonia (every 2–4 h), electrolytes, glucose, ABG, plasma amino acids, BP.
drugs.adverse_effects: Hyperchloraemic acidosis, hyperkalaemia, hyperphosphataemia, vasodilation / hypotension on rapid load.

review.notes: Initiate only with metabolic medicine team.

doses.dose_per_kg_per_dose: 2.5–5 mg/kg/day of colistin BASE in 2–4 divided doses
doses.max_per_day_all_routes: 5 mg/kg/day colistin base activity (normal renal function)
doses.comments: Dosing terminology is confusing — colistin base activity (CBA) ≈ 30 mg of CMS = 1 million IU. Document the unit.

drugs.monitoring: RFT (daily), urine output, electrolytes, neuro status, audiology.
drugs.adverse_effects: Nephrotoxicity (dose-related), neurotoxicity (paraesthesia, ataxia, neuromuscular blockade), bronchospasm (nebulised), eosinophilia.
drugs.contraindications: Severe renal impairment without level monitoring; hypersensitivity.

review.notes: Reserve for confirmed MDR / XDR gram-negatives. Combine with another active agent.

doses.comments: Test for HBV before / during initiation — if HBV+, may flare on FTC withdrawal.

drugs.monitoring: HIV viral load, LFTs, RFT, HBsAg.
drugs.adverse_effects: Headache, nausea, hyperpigmentation (rare in neonates), lactic acidosis (rare).

drugs.monitoring: Continuous BP, HR, ECG.
drugs.adverse_effects: Hypotension, bradycardia, bronchospasm (rare in selective β1), IV-site irritation.
drugs.contraindications: Decompensated heart failure, sinus bradycardia, AV block, hypersensitivity.

review.notes: Half-life ~9 min — easy to titrate quickly.

doses.loading_dose_per_kg: Start 1 g/kg/day; advance by 0.5–1 g/kg/day to goal 3 g/kg/day
doses.comments: 20% emulsion is preferred over 10% (less plasma triglyceride). SMOFlipid / mixed-oil emulsions reduce PNALD risk vs pure soybean Intralipid.

drugs.monitoring: Serum triglycerides (target < 250 mg/dL preterm, < 400 term), bilirubin, LFTs.
drugs.adverse_effects: Hypertriglyceridaemia, cholestasis (PNALD with prolonged use), allergic reactions, sepsis with line contamination.

review.notes: SMOFlipid (mixed-oil) preferred over pure soybean Intralipid in preterm requiring PN > 7–14 days.

drugs.category: Oral iron — anaemia of prematurity prevention

doses.comments: Start by 2 weeks of age. ELBW (< 1000 g) may need 4 mg/kg/day.

drugs.monitoring: Hb, ferritin, transferrin saturation.
drugs.adverse_effects: GI upset, constipation, dark stools, tooth discolouration (rinse mouth), oxidative stress at high doses.

review.notes: Formula-fed term infants get adequate iron from formula. Breast-fed term: start by 4–6 months.

doses.comments: Increase interval if renal dysfunction.

drugs.monitoring: CBC (myelosuppression), LFTs, RFT, drug level (peak 50–100 mcg/mL, trough 25–50; toxic > 100).
drugs.adverse_effects: Bone marrow suppression (dose-related, reversible), hepatotoxicity, GI upset.
drugs.contraindications: Renal impairment without level monitoring; severe hepatic impairment.

review.notes: Always combined with amphotericin B for CNS fungal disease.

doses.duration: ≥ 6 weeks (longer in some cases). Switch to oral valganciclovir when feasible.
doses.comments: Reduce dose by 50% for ANC < 500.

drugs.monitoring: CBC weekly (neutropenia, thrombocytopenia), LFTs, RFT, viral load, audiology.
drugs.adverse_effects: Myelosuppression (neutropenia in 60–80%), nephrotoxicity, hepatotoxicity, IV-site phlebitis, mutagenicity / carcinogenicity (animal data).
drugs.black_box_warning: Granulocytopenia, anaemia, thrombocytopenia, mutagenic and carcinogenic potential, possible impairment of fertility. Pregnancy category C.

review.notes: Valganciclovir 16 mg/kg q12h PO is preferred over IV ganciclovir for the entire 6-month course in symptomatic congenital CMV (Kimberlin 2015 NEJM).

doses.dose_per_kg_per_dose: Engerix-B 10 mcg (0.5 mL) OR Recombivax HB 5 mcg (0.5 mL)
doses.comments: BW < 2 kg: give 4 total doses (birth + 1, 2–3, 6 months).

doses.dose_per_kg_per_dose: Engerix-B 10 mcg (0.5 mL) OR Recombivax HB 5 mcg (0.5 mL)
doses.frequency: Within 24 h of birth (if BW ≥ 2 kg) ; defer to 1 month chronological age (if BW < 2 kg)
doses.comments: Standard 3-dose series: 0–1–6 months for ≥ 2 kg.

drugs.monitoring: Anti-HBs titre at 9–12 months for high-risk infants.
drugs.adverse_effects: Local injection-site soreness, mild fever; very rare anaphylaxis.
drugs.contraindications: Severe hypersensitivity to vaccine components or yeast.

review.notes: Birth dose is universal in India per NIP, regardless of maternal status.

doses.indication: Symptomatic bradycardia / heart block (when pacing unavailable)
doses.comments: Titrate to HR. Correct acidosis first.

drugs.monitoring: Continuous ECG + HR + BP, urine output.
drugs.adverse_effects: Tachyarrhythmia, hypotension (β2-mediated vasodilation), hyperglycaemia, increased myocardial oxygen demand.

review.notes: Largely replaced by transcutaneous / transvenous pacing.

doses.comments: Test for HBV first — risk of HBV flare on withdrawal.

drugs.monitoring: HIV viral load, LFTs, CBC, HBsAg.
drugs.adverse_effects: Headache, GI upset, lactic acidosis (rare), pancreatitis (rare).

drugs.monitoring: Watch for infection (NEC, pneumonia).
drugs.adverse_effects: Same class effects as pantoprazole — increased C. diff, NEC, pneumonia, hypomagnesaemia (chronic).

review.notes: Use shortest course possible — long-term PPI in preterm has been associated with NEC / sepsis.

doses.comments: Maintenance: 10–50 mcg/kg/min IV continuous (preterm — lowest dose).

drugs.monitoring: Continuous ECG, BP, neuro status; serum lidocaine level (target 1.5–5 mcg/mL; toxic > 5).
drugs.adverse_effects: CNS toxicity (drowsiness, twitching, seizure), AV block, hypotension, methaemoglobinaemia (rare).
drugs.contraindications: Stokes-Adams syndrome, severe sinoatrial / AV block.

review.notes: Amiodarone is preferred over lidocaine in PALS 2020 for paediatric pulseless VT/VF.

doses.indication: Refractory neonatal seizure (3rd-line, when phenobarb + phenytoin fail)
doses.dose_per_kg_per_min_range: Maintenance: 6 mg/kg/hr × 6 h, then 4 mg/kg/hr × 12 h, then 2 mg/kg/hr × 12 h (taper)
doses.comments: Total ~ 30 hours. Avoid concurrent phenytoin (combined Na-channel toxicity).

drugs.monitoring: Continuous EEG (seizure response), ECG (arrhythmia, QT), BP.
drugs.adverse_effects: Cardiac arrhythmia (proarrhythmic at high levels), CNS toxicity, methaemoglobinaemia.
drugs.contraindications: Concurrent phenytoin (relative — avoid combination); severe AV block; preterm or hypothermia (clearance reduced — risk of accumulation).

review.notes: Use protocol-driven taper — abrupt stop NOT recommended.

doses.comments: Correct fluid + electrolyte imbalances first. Caution in renal impairment.

drugs.monitoring: Serum osmolality (target ≤ 320 mOsm/kg), urine output, electrolytes (Na, K), RFT, ICP if monitored.
drugs.adverse_effects: Volume overload (initial), then dehydration / hyperosmolar AKI, rebound ICP, electrolyte derangements.
drugs.contraindications: Anuria, severe pulmonary oedema, intracranial bleed (relative — discuss with neurosurgery).

review.notes: Hypertonic 3% saline is the preferred osmotherapy in many neonatal centres now.

doses.indication: Feeding intolerance / GERD (LIMITED ROLE in neonates)
doses.comments: TABLET form NOT recommended in paeds — methaemoglobinaemia + tardive dyskinesia risk. Avoid prolonged use.

drugs.monitoring: Watch for extrapyramidal signs (especially in renal impairment / accumulation), methaemoglobinaemia (cyanosis).
drugs.adverse_effects: Extrapyramidal symptoms (acute dystonia, oculogyric crisis), tardive dyskinesia (chronic), methaemoglobinaemia.
drugs.black_box_warning: Tardive dyskinesia with chronic use (> 12 weeks). Can be irreversible.

review.notes: Erythromycin (10 mg/kg q6h) is preferred prokinetic in neonates — fewer EPS effects.

doses.frequency: PMA-based: ≤ 29 wk: q24h ; 30–36 wk: q12h ; 37–44 wk: q8h ; ≥ 45 wk: q6h

drugs.monitoring: Clinical response; CBC, LFTs.
drugs.adverse_effects: GI upset, peripheral neuropathy (chronic), seizures (rare), disulfiram-like reaction with ethanol.

drugs.monitoring: LFTs, CBC.
drugs.adverse_effects: Transaminitis, infusion reactions (rare), hyperbilirubinaemia (in neonates).

review.notes: Preferred echinocandin for neonates — most published PK / safety data.

doses.dose_per_kg_per_dose: Apply small amount to affected area

drugs.monitoring: Clinical response.
drugs.adverse_effects: Local irritation, rare allergic dermatitis.

review.notes: Watch for resistance with prolonged / repeated use.

doses.comments: ALWAYS pretreat with atropine 0.02 mg/kg or glycopyrrolate to block muscarinic effects.

drugs.monitoring: HR, RR, secretions, train-of-four, muscle tone.
drugs.adverse_effects: Bradycardia, bronchospasm, increased secretions, abdominal cramps, miosis.

review.notes: Sugammadex (where available) preferred for rocuronium / vecuronium reversal — neostigmine reverses any non-depolariser.

doses.comments: Onset within minutes; full effect ~ 50 hours (adult data — neonatal kinetics may differ).

drugs.monitoring: Continuous BP, HR, ECG.
drugs.adverse_effects: Hypotension, reflex tachycardia, headache, IV-site phlebitis.
drugs.contraindications: Severe aortic stenosis, advanced AV block.

review.notes: Useful for sustained BP control where esmolol is unsuitable (e.g. asthma).

doses.dose_per_kg_per_min_range: 0.2–2 mcg/kg/min usual; higher rates may be required
doses.comments: Titrate every 30 min to target BP. PediAid Emergency NICU/PICU calculator handles dilution.

drugs.monitoring: Continuous BP, HR, ECG, urine output, perfusion, IV site (extravasation → necrosis).
drugs.adverse_effects: Tachyarrhythmia, peripheral / mesenteric / renal vasoconstriction, hyperglycaemia, IV infiltration → severe necrosis.
drugs.incompatibilities: Do NOT mix with sodium bicarbonate (inactivates).

review.notes: Treat extravasation with phentolamine 1 mg/mL local infiltration.

doses.comments: Apply half dose to each side of mouth via dropper. Avoid feeding for 5–10 min after.

drugs.monitoring: Resolution of thrush.
drugs.adverse_effects: Negligible at oral / topical doses (poorly absorbed).

review.notes: Some Indian units use oral fluconazole 6 mg/kg/day twice weekly for invasive candidiasis prophylaxis instead of nystatin in extreme preterm.

drugs.monitoring: Watch for infection (NEC, pneumonia).
drugs.adverse_effects: Class — increased C. diff, NEC, pneumonia, hypomagnesaemia (chronic).

review.notes: Use shortest course possible.

drugs.monitoring: CBC, LFTs (transaminitis), RFT.
drugs.adverse_effects: Hepatic transaminitis (more than nafcillin), neutropenia, rash, interstitial nephritis.

review.notes: Cefazolin is alternative for MSSA. Vancomycin if MRSA.

doses.indication: RSV prophylaxis (high-risk preterm / CHD / chronic lung disease)
doses.comments: Continue throughout RSV season once started. Discontinue if breakthrough RSV hospitalisation. Consider post-CPB / ECMO redose. NIRSEVIMAB is preferred where available.

drugs.monitoring: Clinical signs of RSV infection.
drugs.adverse_effects: Local injection site reactions, mild fever; rare anaphylaxis.

review.notes: Nirsevimab (single-dose, longer-acting) is replacing palivizumab globally where available.

doses.comments: Test dose 0.02 mg/kg first to assess responsiveness.

drugs.monitoring: Train-of-four, ventilator synchrony, eye and pressure-area care.
drugs.adverse_effects: Tachycardia (vagolytic effect), prolonged paralysis with hepatic / renal impairment, increased BP.

review.notes: Long duration — useful for sustained paralysis. Vecuronium / rocuronium have less hepatic accumulation.

doses.frequency: Repeat as needed; or by infusion in burst-suppression protocol

drugs.monitoring: RR, SpO₂, BP, EEG (burst suppression in status), level (target 20–40 mcg/mL for status).
drugs.adverse_effects: Respiratory depression, hypotension, prolonged sedation, immunosuppression.
drugs.contraindications: Severe respiratory depression without ventilation; porphyria.

review.notes: Reserve for refractory status epilepticus. Phenobarbital is first-line.

doses.route: Subcutaneous (local infiltration around the affected area)
doses.dose_per_kg_per_dose: Inject 0.5 mg/mL solution into affected area; 1–5 mL total depending on size of infiltrate

drugs.monitoring: BP (hypotension after systemic absorption), perfusion of affected limb, infiltrate resolution.
drugs.adverse_effects: Hypotension, tachycardia, GI upset (if systemic absorption), nasal congestion.

review.notes: Crucial NICU bedside drug — must be available wherever catecholamines are infused. Dilute 5 mg vial in 9 mL NS = 0.5 mg/mL.

doses.comments: Increases SVR and reduces R-to-L shunting across VSD.

doses.dose_per_kg_per_min_range: 2–5 mcg/kg/min, titrate to oxygenation while avoiding hypotension

doses.dose_per_kg_per_dose: 1 drop in eye ≥ 10 min before exam
doses.comments: Use only 2.5 % (NEVER 10 %). Apply pressure to lacrimal sac during and 2 min after to minimise systemic absorption.

drugs.monitoring: BP, HR.
drugs.adverse_effects: Hypertension, reflex bradycardia, decreased renal / GI perfusion at high dose. Systemic effects of ophthalmic 10 % (HTN, hypothermia).

drugs.monitoring: CBC, RFT, electrolytes (Na load).
drugs.adverse_effects: Same as pip-tazo; high sodium load (1.85 mEq Na per gram).

review.notes: Plain piperacillin is rarely available now; pip-tazo is the standard formulation.

doses.indication: Reverse heparin (UFH) — by time elapsed since last heparin
doses.dose_per_kg_per_dose: < 30 min since heparin: 1 mg per 100 U heparin given ; 30–60 min: 0.5–0.75 mg per 100 U ; 60–120 min: 0.375–0.5 mg per 100 U ; > 120 min: 0.25–0.375 mg per 100 U
Dalteparin: 1 mg per 100 anti-Xa IU. Enoxaparin: 1 mg per 1 mg of enoxaparin given < 8 h ago (0.5 mg per 1 mg if 8–12 h ago)

drugs.monitoring: Continuous BP, HR; aPTT 5 min and 2–4 h after; ACT (ECMO).
drugs.adverse_effects: Hypotension, bradycardia, anaphylaxis (especially in fish allergy / prior protamine exposure / NPH-insulin patients), pulmonary hypertension.

review.notes: Avoid rapid push — risk of histamine-mediated hypotension.

doses.comments: Lower 0.12 mg / 0.02 mL effective in pilot studies. Premedicate with topical anaesthetic (proparacaine / tetracaine) and ophthalmic antiseptic (povidone iodine 5 % or 10 %). Topical antibiotic drops × 7 days post-procedure.

drugs.monitoring: Serial retinal exam; systemic monitoring (less systemic exposure than bevacizumab).
drugs.adverse_effects: Endophthalmitis (rare), retinal detachment, transient IOP rise.

review.notes: Ranibizumab has lower systemic VEGF suppression than bevacizumab — preferred where availability and cost permit.

doses.comments: Confirm retinal vascularisation in extreme preterm before starting.
Highly variable PK — careful titration with O₂ and BP monitoring.

drugs.monitoring: BP (systemic hypotension), oxygenation, retinal exam (extremely preterm — not before retinal vascularisation).
drugs.adverse_effects: Hypotension, retinal effects, gastrointestinal, priapism (rare in newborn).
drugs.contraindications: Concurrent nitrates, severe hypotension, retinal vascular immaturity (extreme preterm).

review.notes: Used as bridge / adjunct to iNO. STRIDER trial showed no benefit and possible harm for PPROM-associated pulmonary hypoplasia.

doses.comments: Lactated Ringer's is acceptable alternative. Reassess for hepatomegaly before further boluses.

drugs.monitoring: Serum Na, urine output, weight, fluid balance.
drugs.adverse_effects: Volume overload, hypernatraemia, hyperchloraemic acidosis (large volumes).

review.notes: Hypertonic saline (3 %) — for symptomatic hyponatraemia, dose 3–5 mL/kg over 15–30 min (raises serum Na ~ 3 mEq/L).

doses.frequency: every 12 hours, increased gradually under monitoring
doses.comments: INITIATE in hospital with continuous ECG × 3 days. Baseline QTc must be < 450 ms.

drugs.monitoring: Continuous ECG (QTc, AV block), K, Mg, RFT.
drugs.adverse_effects: Torsades de pointes (especially with hypoK / hypoMg), bradycardia, AV block, hypotension, bronchospasm.
drugs.contraindications: Baseline QTc > 450 ms, severe sinus bradycardia, AV block (without pacemaker), bronchial asthma, decompensated heart failure.
drugs.renal_adjustment: Required — calculate CrCl to set interval.

review.notes: Initiate only in hospital with cardiologist input.

doses.indication: Procedural pain (heel prick, IV insertion, NG insertion, injections)
doses.frequency: 2 minutes before painful procedure ; offer pacifier after
doses.comments: Effect peaks at 2 min, lasts ~ 5 min.

drugs.monitoring: Pain score (NIPS, PIPP, FLACC).
drugs.adverse_effects: Negligible. No glucose effect at small volumes.
drugs.contraindications: NPO status (relative — avoid in suspected NEC, recent surgery).

cross_checks.source: Cochrane review — Sucrose for procedural pain

review.notes: Combine with non-nutritive sucking (pacifier) and skin-to-skin / facilitated tucking for maximal analgesia.

drugs.monitoring: LFTs (especially conjugated bilirubin), GGT.
drugs.adverse_effects: Diarrhoea (most common), abdominal pain, rash.
drugs.contraindications: Acute cholangitis, complete biliary obstruction, hepatic failure.

review.notes: Common adjunct in PN-dependent infants with cholestasis.

doses.dose_per_kg_per_dose: Hold if ANC < 500 (recheck) ; resume at 50 % when ANC > 750 ; discontinue if ANC drops < 500 again

drugs.monitoring: CBC weekly × 6 weeks then monthly (neutropenia in ~ 20 %), LFTs, RFT, audiology, viral load.
drugs.adverse_effects: Neutropenia (most common), thrombocytopenia, hepatotoxicity, mutagenic / carcinogenic potential.
drugs.contraindications: ANC < 500 (without dose reduction), platelets < 25k, severe renal impairment.
drugs.black_box_warning: Bone marrow suppression, mutagenic / carcinogenic potential.

review.notes: Oral valganciclovir × 6 months is the preferred course for symptomatic congenital CMV (improves audiologic and neurodevelopmental outcomes per Kimberlin 2015 NEJM).

india_formulations.strength: Per 1 mL: Vit A 250 mcg RAE / D 400 IU / C 50 mg / E 5 mg / B-complex (varies by brand)
india_formulations.notes: Compositions vary by brand — verify each ingredient against requirement.

doses.indication: Routine supplementation — preterm and exclusively breastfed term

drugs.monitoring: Routine clinical.
drugs.adverse_effects: GI upset, hypervitaminosis (rare at recommended doses).

review.notes: Avoid duplicating with separate vitamin D / iron supplements.
